item.title: Neumorphism 网站 + Github API（第 4 部分） | Neumorphism Website + Github API (Part 4)
item.description:
贝内迪克特·沙赫纳

发表于 7 月 19 日

嘿亲爱的开发者，

在这篇文章中，我想介绍一种在 JavaScript 中检查 API 状态的简单方法。 当与外部 API（例如我们）交互并希望确保其可访问时，这尤其有用。

你喜欢我的文章吗？ 然后我会很高兴收到点赞和评论！

该项目展示了我的 GitHub 存储库，并允许您探索我一直在从事的项目。 它使用 GitHub API 获取项目数据并以用户友好的格式显示。

为什么它可能部分不起作用！❗

如果您喜欢我的项目，我将感谢您的支持与星星⭐！

介绍

欢迎来到我的 GitHub 项目展示！ 该项目重点介绍了我一直在开发的存储库，并提供了一个具有视觉吸引力且用户友好的界面来探索它们。 它利用 GitHub API 获取项目数据，并结合各种设计效果来增强整体体验。

功能性

获取 GitHub 项目

它检索存储库及其详细信息......

挑战

假设我们正在开发一个与 GitHub API 交互的 Web 应用程序，以获取有关用户及其项目的信息。 在开始使用该应用程序之前，我们需要确保 GitHub API 正常工作。 否则，当 API 无法访问时，我们的应用程序可能会失败或出现不可预测的行为。

解决方案

以下是在 JavaScript 中检查 API 状态的简单方法：

  1. 创建一个进行简单 API 调用的函数。 例如，我们可以使用fetch方法向 API 发送请求。

     async function checkAPIStatus() { try { const response = await fetch("https://api.github.com/"); if (response.status === 200) { // API is reachable console.log("🟢 GitHub API is working"); } else { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); } } catch (error) { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); } }

在 JavaScript 中检查 API 状态：一个简单的方法

嘿亲爱的开发者，

在这篇文章中，我想介绍一种在 JavaScript 中检查 API 状态的简单方法。 当与外部 API 交互并希望在采取任何进一步操作之前确保其可访问时，这尤其有用。

挑战

假设我们正在开发一个与 GitHub API 交互的 Web 应用程序，以获取有关用户及其项目的信息。 在开始使用该应用程序之前，我们需要确保 GitHub API 正常工作。 否则，当 API 无法访问时，我们的应用程序可能会失败或出现不可预测的行为。

解决方案

以下是在 JavaScript 中检查 API 状态的简单方法：

  1. 创建一个进行简单 API 调用的函数。 例如，我们可以使用fetch方法向 API 发送请求。
 async function checkAPIStatus() { try { const response = await fetch("https://api.github.com/"); if (response.status === 200) { // API is reachable console.log("🟢 GitHub API is working"); } else { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); } } catch (error) { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); } }

调用 checkAPIStatus() 函数并检查 API 的状态。

根据状态代码，您可以决定如何处理 API 可用性。 如果 API 可以访问，您可以显示成功消息；如果无法访问，则可以抛出错误消息。 结论

检查 API 状态对于开发强大的 Web 应用程序来说是一件简单但重要的事情。 通过检查 API 的状态，我们可以确保我们的应用程序顺利运行并为用户提供积极的体验。

您可以将此方法合并到您的 Web 项目中，以确保外部 API 在采取任何进一步步骤之前正常工作。 这有助于及早发现潜在错误并改善用户体验。

我希望这个方法对你有所帮助，帮助你构建可靠的Web应用程序。 快乐编码！ 谢谢，
施本尼迪克特

一旦被暂停，schbenedikt 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，schbenedikt 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，schbenedikt 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 schbenedikt 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

使 schbenedikt 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 schbenedikt 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Benedikt Schächner

Posted on Jul 19

Hey dear developers,

In this post, I would like to introduce a simple way to check the status of an API in JavaScript. This can be especially useful when interacting with an external API (like us) and want to make sure it's reachable.

Do you like my articles? Then I would be happy about a like and a comment!

This project showcases my GitHub repositories and allows you to explore the projects I've been working on. It uses the GitHub API to fetch the project data and displays it in a user-friendly format.

❗THERE IS A LIMITED TOKEN REQUEST; WHY IT MAY BE PARTIALLY NOT WORKING!❗

If you like my project, I would appreciate your support with a star ⭐!

Introduction

Welcome to my GitHub Projects showcase! This project highlights the repositories I've been working on and provides a visually appealing and user-friendly interface to explore them. It utilizes the GitHub API to fetch the project data and incorporates various design effects to enhance the overall experience.

Functionality

Fetching GitHub Projects

The getGitHubProjects(username) function leverages the power of the GitHub API to fetch the project data associated with a given username. It retrieves the repositories and their details in a…

The challenge

Let's imagine we're developing a web application that interacts with the GitHub API to get information about users and their projects. Before we start using the application, we want to make sure that the GitHub API is working properly. Otherwise, our application could fail or behave unpredictably when the API is unreachable.

The solution

Here's a simple way to check the status of an API in JavaScript:

  1. Create a function that makes a simple API call. For example, we can use the fetch method to send a request to the API.

    async function checkAPIStatus() { try { const response = await fetch("https://api.github.com/"); if (response.status === 200) { // API is reachable console.log("🟢 GitHub API is working"); } else { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); } } catch (error) { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); }
}

Checking API status in JavaScript: A simple method

Hey dear developers,

In this post, I would like to introduce a simple way to check the status of an API in JavaScript. This can be especially useful when interacting with an external API and want to make sure it's reachable before taking any further action.

The challenge

Let's imagine we're developing a web application that interacts with the GitHub API to get information about users and their projects. Before we start using the application, we want to make sure that the GitHub API is working properly. Otherwise, our application could fail or behave unpredictably when the API is unreachable.

The solution

Here's a simple way to check the status of an API in JavaScript:

  1. Create a function that makes a simple API call. For example, we can use the fetch method to send a request to the API. 
async function checkAPIStatus() { try { const response = await fetch("https://api.github.com/"); if (response.status === 200) { // API is reachable console.log("🟢 GitHub API is working"); } else { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); } } catch (error) { // API is not reachable console.error("🔴 GitHub API is not available"); }
}

Call the checkAPIStatus() function and check the status of the API.

Based on the status code, you can then decide how to handle API availability. You could show a success message if the API is reachable, or throw an error message if it's unreachable. Conclusion

Checking API status is a simple but important thing to do to develop a robust web application. By checking the status of the API, we can ensure that our application works smoothly and provides users with a positive experience.

You can incorporate this method into your web projects to ensure external APIs are working properly before taking any further steps. This helps to catch potential errors early and improve user experience.

I hope this method is helpful to you and helps you build reliable web applications. Happy coding! 🚀

Thanks,
schBenedikt

item.title: 第 8 天：限制 api 调用 | Day 8: Limit api calls
item.description:
达兰·加内桑

发表于 7 月 19 日

创建一个名为limitCalls的函数，该函数接受一个函数和最大调用次数作为参数。 该函数应该返回一个闭包，该闭包仅调用原始函数达到指定的最大次数。

 function logMessage(message) { console.log(message); } const limitedLogMessage = limitCalls(logMessage, 3); limitedLogMessage('Hello'); // Output: Hello limitedLogMessage('World'); // Output: World limitedLogMessage('OpenAI'); // Output: Challenge limitedLogMessage('Extra call'); // Output: Maximum number of calls reached!

Dharan Ganesan

Posted on Jul 19

Create a function called limitCalls that takes a function and a maximum number of calls as arguments. The function should return a closure that only invokes the original function up to the specified maximum number of times.

function logMessage(message) { console.log(message);
} const limitedLogMessage = limitCalls(logMessage, 3); limitedLogMessage('Hello'); // Output: Hello
limitedLogMessage('World'); // Output: World
limitedLogMessage('OpenAI'); // Output: Challenge
limitedLogMessage('Extra call'); // Output: Maximum number of calls reached!

item.title: 使用 Next.js 构建产品组合：迁移到 App Router | Building Portfolio with Next.js: Migrate to App Router
item.description:
嘿朋友们👋，

正如之前的文章中提到的，我错误地阅读了Pages Router文档，而不是App Router 。 为了一致地使用应用程序路由器，我必须将pages文件夹内的文件（路由）移动到app文件夹中。

我学到的是

服务器和客户端组件

  • 这意味着默认情况下，React 在服务器端渲染整个应用程序。 使用 Pages Router 时，React 将整个应用程序呈现在客户端。

  • 我们可以在 App Router 中组合服务器和客户端组件。

  • 使用服务器组件，客户端 JavaScript 包的大小会减小，因此初始页面加载速度会更快。 阅读为什么要提供组件？ 和文档中的渲染以获得更全面的解释。

  • 与用户交互性和 React hooks 相关的任何内容仍然需要在客户端渲染。

  • 选择服务器组件时，请考虑将敏感信息保留在服务器上、处理数据（获取数据、直接访问后端资源）或处理较大的依赖项。

路线

  • 所有路由都位于应用程序路由器功能中的app程序文件夹下。

  • 如果我们想公开路由，每个文件夹都应该有一个page.js文件。

路线组

  • 我们可以通过将路由分组到一个特殊的文件夹中来组织路由，该文件夹中的路由不会包含在 URL 路径中。

  • 为什么我们要对路线进行分组？

    我们的应用程序或网站将来可能会有许多具有不同目的的页面。 例如，电子商务网站通常具有公众可以消费的页面和只能通过身份验证才能访问的页面。 我们可以将路线组织到文件夹中，例如(shop)文件夹、 (dashboard)文件夹等。

  • 创建路由组的约定是将文件夹名称括在括号中； 我做了什么

    • 我在app程序文件夹中为每个路线（关于、博客和联系人）创建了一个文件夹。

    • 我将pages中的所有文件移至app文件夹并将它们放入其文件夹中。

    • 我将所有文件的名称更改为page.js以供公开访问。

    • 因为现在我有三个公共文件夹（路由），并且将来可能会有一个仪表板，所以我将路由分组在(websitePages)文件夹中——我现在想不出更好的名称 😆。

    所以目前， app文件夹结构是这样的：

     . └── app/ ├── (websitePages)/ │ ├── about/ │ │ └── page.js │ ├── blog/ │ │ └── page.js │ └── contact/ │ └── page.js ├── global.css ├── layout.js └── page.js

    一些想法

    在我将路由移至应用程序文件夹之前，HTML 具有良好的样式。 但当我将它们移动到应用程序文件夹后，样式就消失了。 标题与段落大小相同。 我提供了一个 GIF 来比较 App Router 和 Pages Router。 我还不知道是什么原因造成的。 如果您知道发生了什么事，请在下面发表评论😀。

    下一步计划

    • 创建导航栏和页脚。

    项目链接

    资源

    感谢您的阅读！ 最后，您可以在Twitter 、 Mastodon和BlueSky上找到我。 让我们联系吧！ 一旦被暂停，adiatiayu 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，adiatiayu 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，adiatiayu 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 adiatiayu 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 adiatiayu：

使 adiatiayu 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 adiatiayu 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hey Friends 👋,

As mentioned in the earlier post, I mistakenly read the Pages Router docs instead of the App Router. To use the App Router consistently, I have to move the files (routes) inside the pages folder into the app folder.

What I Learned

Server and Client Components

  • Next.js 13 introduces App Router built on React Server Component. It means that by default, React renders the whole application server-side. While with Pages Router, React renders the whole application client-side.

  • We can combine server and client components in App Router.

  • With server components, the client-side JavaScript bundle size is reduced, so the initial page loads faster. Read the section Why Serve Components? and Rendering in the docs for a more thorough explanation.

  • Anything related to the user's interactivity and React hooks still needs to render on the client side.

  • Think of keeping sensitive information on the server, working with data (fetching data, accessing backend resources directly), or working with large dependencies when choosing the server component.

Routes

  • All routes are live under the app folder in the App Router feature.

  • Each folder in the app folder defines a route.

  • Each folder should have a page.js file if we want to make the route public.

Route Groups

  • We can organize routes by grouping them in a special folder where the route will not be included in the URL path.

  • Why do we want to group the routes?

    Our app or website might have many pages in the future with different purposes. For example, an e-commerce website usually has pages that the public can consume and pages that can only be accessed with authentication. We can organize the routes into folders, e.g., (shop) folder, (dashboard) folder, etc.

  • The convention to create a route group is by wrapping the folder name in parenthesis; (folderName).

What I Did

  • I created a folder for each route—about, blog, and contact—in the app folder.

  • I moved all files in the pages to the app folder and put them in their folder.

  • I changed all files' names to page.js to be accessed publicly.

  • Because now I have three public folders—routes—and probably will have a dashboard in the future, I grouped the routes in the (websitePages) folder—I can't think of a better name for now 😆.

So currently, the app folder structure is like this:

.
└── app/
    ├── (websitePages)/
    │   ├── about/
    │   │   └── page.js
    │   ├── blog/
    │   │   └── page.js
    │   └── contact/
    │       └── page.js
    ├── global.css
    ├── layout.js
    └── page.js

Some Thoughts

The HTML had good styling before I moved the routes into the app folder. The <h1> and <h2> have the proper size for headings.

But the styling is gone after I moved them to the app folder. The headings are the same size as the paragraph. I provide a GIF for comparing App Router and Pages Router. I don't know yet what caused it. If you know what happened, drop a comment below 😀.

Next Plan

  • Create a navbar and a footer.

Project Link

Resources

🖼️ Credit cover image: unDraw

Thank you for reading! Last, you can find me on Twitter, Mastodon, and BlueSky. Let's connect! 😊

item.title: 使用 AWS 网络资源图了解您的架构 | Understand your architecture with AWS Network Resource Map
item.description:
在当今日益复杂的云环境中，清楚地了解您的网络基础设施对于高效管理和故障排除至关重要。 在本文中，您将了解网络资源图如何提供​​有关网络配置的宝贵见解并帮助您优化 AWS 云基础设施。

让我们从基础知识开始，什么是网络资源映射：

网络资源地图是 AWS 提供的一项功能，可提供网络基础设施的可视化表示。
它以图形方式概述了 AWS 云中的 Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)、子网、安全组和网络流量。 该图提供了网络资源、它们的互连以及数据流的高级视图，使您能够理解网络架构的复杂性。

在哪里可以找到我的资源图：

您可以进入VPC控制面板，选择VPC并进入网络资源映射选项卡。 这呈现了一个交互式动态地图，其中资源表示为节点，它们之间的连接显示为线条。

概括

有了它，您将更容易理解各个基础设施元素是如何连接的。 更容易理解子网、NAT 网关、互联网网关和网关端点之间的流量。

您还可以在我的视频中找到资源映射的简短提及，其中我使用专用 NAT 网关解决了 IP 地址重叠的问题。

一旦暂停，aws-builders 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦取消暂停，aws-builders 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 aws-builders：

使 aws-builders 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

In today's increasingly complex cloud environments, having a clear understanding of your network infrastructure is crucial for efficient management and troubleshooting. AWS cloud offers a powerful tool called the Network Resource Map, which allows users to visualize and comprehend their network architecture within the AWS cloud.

In this article, you'll learn how the Network Resource Map provides valuable insights into your network configuration and helps you optimize your AWS cloud infrastructure.

Let's start with the basics, what is a Network Resource Map:

The Network Resource Map is a feature provided by AWS that offers a visual representation of your network infrastructure.
It presents a graphical overview of your virtual private cloud (VPC), subnets, security groups, and network traffic flows within the AWS cloud. This map provides a high-level view of your network resources, their interconnections, and the flow of data, empowering you to comprehend the complexities of your network architecture.

Where to find my resource map:

You can go to the VPC Dashboard, select the VPC and go to the Network Resource Map tab. This presents an interactive and dynamic map, where resources are represented as nodes, and the connections between them are displayed as lines.

Summary

AWS resource map will be especially useful at the beginning of your adventure in the cloud. Thanks to it, it will be easier for you to understand how the individual infrastructure elements are connected. It will be easier to understand the flow of traffic between subnets, NAT gateways, internet gateway and gateway endpoints.

You can also find a brief mention of Resource Map in my video in which I solve the problem with overlapping IP addresses using a private NAT gateway.

item.title: 使用 React.useMemo() 进行记忆 | Memoization using React.useMemo()
item.description:
首先我们需要了解什么是记忆化。 记忆化是一种优化技术，我们通过缓存相同输入的大量函数调用的结果来加速我们的程序。

简单来说，例如我们有一个输入“2”，我们将它传递给我们的函数，这需要花费很多时间来执行。 现在，如果我们需要再次计算输入“2”的结果，那么我们需要再次执行该函数，并且再次需要那么多时间。

但如果我们在第一次使用记忆化，则不会，如果我们记忆该函数“2”的输出，那么我们只需要从记忆值中获取该值，而不需要再次为“2”执行该函数，这将节省我们执行所需的时间，并使我们的程序更加优化。

在这篇文章中，我们将了解如何使用useMemo React hook

使用Memo()

它接受 2 个参数 - 计算结果的函数和depedencies数组：

 const memoizedValue = useMemo(calculateValue, dependencies)

在初始渲染期间， useMemo(calculateValue, dependencies)调用calculateValue，记忆计算结果并将其返回给组件。

如果依赖项在下一次渲染期间没有更改，则useMemo()不会调用calculateValue ，而是返回记忆值。

但是，如果依赖项在重新渲染期间发生变化，则useMemo()会调用calculateValue ，记住新值并返回它。

 const memoizedResult = useMemo(() => { return heavyFunction(A, B); }, [A, B]);

在这种情况下，我们有两个依赖项 A 和 B，因此对于第一次渲染，它将让heavyFunction运行以获取输出，但在重新渲染中，如果依赖项没有更改，那么它将不会执行该函数，并且将返回记忆的值。

例子

让我们以计算阶乘的经典递归问题为例。

在阶乘中，我们经常有相同的输入，并且在没有记忆的情况下，函数总是继续计算结果，但是如果我们记忆一个值的输出并返回，那么我们将节省大量递归周期，并且只返回记忆的值仅在这一点上。

 function fact(n) { if(n <= 0){ return 1; } return n * fact(n - 1); }

这是使用递归对数字 n 进行阶乘的简单实现。

现在，如果我们有一个完整的组件：

 import { useState, useMemo } from 'react'; export function CalculateFactorial() { const [number, setNumber] = useState(5); const factorial = useMemo(() => fact(number), [number]); const onChange = event => { setNumber(Number(event.target.value)); }; return ( <div> Factorial of <input type="number" value={number} onChange={onChange} /> is {factorial} </div> ); } function fact(n) { console.log('factorial called!'); if(n <= 0){ return 1; } return n * fact(n - 1); }

每次改变数字的值时， factorial called! 已记录到控制台。 这是预期的。

但是，如果我们重新渲染而不改变数字，则factorial called! 未记录到控制台，因为useMemo(() => fact(number), [number])返回记忆的阶乘计算。 从而使我们免于再次调用该函数并重新计算。

您不能在循环或条件内调用它。

我希望现在你了解useMemo在 React 中是如何工作的。
谢谢阅读。



原文:

First we need to understand what memoization is. Memoization is an optimization technique, in which we accelerate our programs by caching the results of heavy function calls for the same inputs.

In simple terms, for example we have an input say '2' and we pass it through our function which takes a lot of time to execute. Now, if we need to calculate the result for input '2' again then we need to perform the function again and it will again take that much time.

But not if we used memoization in the first go, if we memoize the output for '2' of that function then we just need to get that value from our memoized values and don't need to execute that function again for '2', which in term will save us the time it takes for execution, and will make our program much more optimized.

In this post we will understand how to use useMemo React hook

useMemo()

useMemo is a React Hook that lets you cache the result of a calculation between re-renders. It accepts 2 arguments- a function that computes a result, and the depedencies array:

const memoizedValue = useMemo(calculateValue, dependencies)

During initial rendering, useMemo(calculateValue, dependencies) invokes calculateValue, memoizes the calculation result, and returns it to the component.

If the dependencies don't change during the next renderings, then useMemo() doesn't invoke calculateValue, but returns the memoized value.

But if the dependencies change during re-rendering, then useMemo() invokes calculateValue, memoizes the new value, and returns it.

const memoizedResult = useMemo(() => {
  return heavyFunction(A, B);
}, [A, B]);

In this case we have two dependencies A and B, so for the first render it will let the heavyFunction run to get the output, but in the re-renders if the dependencies don't change then it'll not execute the function and will return the memoized value.

Example

Let's take example of a classic recursion problem of calculating a factorial.

In factorial we often have the same input and without memoization the function always keeps on going to calculate the result, but if we memoize the output for a value and return that then we will save a lot of recursion cycles and will just return the memoized value at that point only.

function fact(n) {
  if(n <= 0){
    return 1;
  }
  return n * fact(n - 1);
}

So this is a simple implementation of factorial of a number n using recursion.

Now, if we have a whole component for this:

import { useState, useMemo } from 'react';

export function CalculateFactorial() {
  const [number, setNumber] = useState(5);

  const factorial = useMemo(() => fact(number), [number]);

  const onChange = event => {
    setNumber(Number(event.target.value));
  };

  return (
    <div>
      Factorial of 
      <input type="number" value={number} onChange={onChange} />
      is {factorial}
    </div>
  );
}

function fact(n) {
  console.log('factorial called!');
  if(n <= 0){
    return 1;
  }
  return n * fact(n - 1);
}

Every time you change the value of the number, factorial called! is logged to console. That's expected.

However, if we Re-render without changing the number, factorial called! isn't logged to console because useMemo(() => fact(number), [number]) returns the memoized factorial calculation. And hence saving us from calling the function again and recalculating.

useMemo() is a Hook, so you can only call it at the top level of your component or your own Hooks. You can’t call it inside loops or conditions.

I hope now you understand how useMemo works in React.
Thanks for reading.
item.author: hardiksharma

item.title: 掌握 Laravel：展示您技能的十大培训任务 | Mastering Laravel: Top 10 Training Tasks to Showcase Your Skills
item.description:
作为 Laravel 爱好者，证明您的熟练程度需要的不仅仅是理论知识。 通过实际项目展示您的技能对于在同行和潜在雇主中脱颖而出至关重要。 在本文中，我们提出了十项具有挑战性的培训任务，涵盖了 Laravel 的各个方面，从用户身份验证到实时聊天应用程序。 完成这些任务不仅可以提高您的技能，还可以作为令人印象深刻的作品集来展示您的专业知识。

用户认证系统
用户身份验证的坚实基础对于任何 Web 应用程序都至关重要。 创建安全且用户友好的身份验证系统，具有用户注册、登录、密码重置和电子邮件验证等功能。 强调保护用户凭据的安全措施和最佳实践。

增删改查应用
实施成熟的 CRUD（创建、读取、更新、删除）应用程序来管理特定域。 该项目将展示您设计高效数据库模式和构建交互式用户界面以实现无缝数据管理的能力。

文件上传与管理
开发一个文件上传和管理系统，允许用户上传文件、对文件进行分类以及对文件进行CRUD操作。 强调正确的验证、安全的文件存储和高效的文件检索。

实时聊天应用程序
使用 Laravel Broadcasting 和 Pusher 构建实时聊天应用程序。 允许用户即时发送和接收消息，展示您对事件广播和处理实时更新的理解。

为特定领域（例如博客、电子商务平台或社交媒体应用程序）创建强大的 RESTful API。 实施 JWT 身份验证并为各种操作（例如创建、更新和删除资源）提供记录良好的端点。

任务计划程序和 Cron 作业
利用 Laravel 的任务计划程序和 cron 作业自动执行重复性任务，例如定期发送电子邮件或按计划的时间间隔处理数据。 展示您有效管理重复任务的能力。

基于角色的访问控制 (RBAC)
实施基于角色的访问控制，有效管理用户权限。 创建一个系统，其中管理员、经理和普通用户对各种功能具有不同级别的访问权限。

搜索功能
使用 Laravel Scout 和 Algolia 或 Elasticsearch 开发强大的搜索功能。 允许用户高效地搜索资源并展示您构建强大搜索系统的能力。

与第三方API集成
将您的应用程序与流行的第三方 API 集成，例如 Google 地图、Twitter 或 Stripe 等支付网关。 展示您在使用外部服务和有效处理 API 响应方面的专业知识。

本地化和多语言支持
通过添加本地化和多语言支持来增强您的应用程序。 使用户能够在不同语言之间切换并为各种内容提供翻译。

结论

掌握 Laravel 需要实践经验和概念的实际应用。 通过完成这十项培训任务，您将展示您利用 Laravel 的全部潜力来设计和构建现实世界的 Web 应用程序的能力。 专注于遵守 Laravel 的最佳实践，维护干净且有组织的代码，并为每项任务提供全面的文档。 多样化的项目组合无疑会给潜在的雇主和客户留下深刻的印象，证明您是一名熟练且有能力的 Laravel 开发人员。 祝您编码愉快，祝您好运，展示您对 Laravel 的掌握！



原文:

Laravel, the popular PHP framework, empowers developers to build elegant and robust web applications. As a Laravel enthusiast, proving your proficiency requires more than theoretical knowledge. Demonstrating your skills through practical projects is vital to stand out among peers and potential employers. In this article, we present ten challenging training tasks that cover various aspects of Laravel, from user authentication to real-time chat applications. Completing these tasks will not only enhance your skills but also serve as an impressive portfolio to showcase your expertise.

User Authentication System
A strong foundation in user authentication is crucial for any web application. Create a secure and user-friendly authentication system with features like user registration, login, password reset, and email verification. Emphasize security measures and best practices to protect user credentials.

CRUD Application
Implement a full-fledged CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) application for managing a specific domain. This project will demonstrate your ability to design efficient database schemas and build interactive user interfaces for seamless data management.

File Upload and Management
Develop a file upload and management system that allows users to upload files, categorize them, and perform CRUD operations on files. Emphasize proper validation, secure file storage, and efficient file retrieval.

Real-Time Chat Application
Build a real-time chat application using Laravel Broadcasting and Pusher. Allow users to send and receive messages instantly, showcasing your understanding of event broadcasting and handling real-time updates.

API Development
Create a robust RESTful API for a specific domain, such as a blog, e-commerce platform, or social media app. Implement JWT authentication and provide well-documented endpoints for various actions, like creating, updating, and deleting resources.

Task Scheduler and Cron Jobs
Leverage Laravel's task scheduler and cron jobs to automate repetitive tasks, such as sending periodic emails or processing data at scheduled intervals. Showcase your proficiency in managing recurring tasks efficiently.

Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
Implement role-based access control to manage user permissions effectively. Create a system where administrators, managers, and regular users have different levels of access to various functionalities.

Search Functionality
Develop a powerful search functionality using Laravel Scout and Algolia or Elasticsearch. Allow users to search for resources efficiently and demonstrate your ability to build robust search systems.

Integration with Third-Party APIs
Integrate your application with popular third-party APIs, such as Google Maps, Twitter, or payment gateways like Stripe. Showcase your expertise in working with external services and handling API responses effectively.

Localization and Multilingual Support
Enhance your application by adding localization and multilingual support. Enable users to switch between different languages and provide translations for various content.

Conclusion

Mastering Laravel requires hands-on experience and practical application of concepts. By completing these ten training tasks, you will showcase your ability to design and build real-world web applications using Laravel's full potential. Focus on adhering to Laravel's best practices, maintaining clean and organized code, and providing comprehensive documentation for each task. A diverse portfolio of projects will undoubtedly impress potential employers and clients, certifying you as a skilled and competent Laravel developer. Happy coding and best of luck in showcasing your Laravel mastery!
